conflict: Marcus's unilateral deal and the resulting power shift — he has distribution leverage the ring can't replace
consequence: Marcus breaks from the ring; the operation is compromised; Harry's origin story reframes the ring as survival, not enterprise
must_land_beats: Agnes intercepts Marcus's accidental text to the shared phone — quantities that exceed anything the ring has moved
Harry's cold fury — the accountant's anger, precise and withholding
Marcus's argument: he bears the real risk, they'd get sympathy while he'd get prison
Power shift: Marcus is the only one who can leave the building
Earl: 'You're not one of us anymore' — it hurts Marcus
Harry's origin story: the pharmacy switch, delayed prescriptions, Agnes in pain for two weeks, the ring started as medication self-preservation
Carmen asks 'What do we do now?' — Harry: 'We wait'

payoffs_to_land.description: Marcus's resentment from his unjust firing, established in Ch 6, surfaces in his argument — he was punished for caring about these people and now they're punishing him for caring about himself

Harry tells the partial origin story — the pharmacy switch, medication delays, Agnes's pain — reframing the ring's founding from enterprise to survival; the final piece (the dangerous drug interaction) is withheld for Ch 18

pressure_source: Marcus's unilateral deal and the resulting power shift — he has distribution leverage the ring can't replace
secondary_character_beats: Marcus's hurt at Earl's rejection reveals that belonging to this group matters more to him than the money — but not enough to stop him
state_shift: Marcus breaks from the ring; the operation is compromised; Harry's origin story reframes the ring as survival, not enterprise
